title: [CI:DOCS] Improve swagger+CORS metadata docs
body:
Fixes #6466 - The CORS metadata only refererred to `http://docs.podman.io` and not `https://docs.podman.io`.  CORS treats these as completely different sites.

Note: For client (user's web-browser) security, maintaining the CORS configuration should not be automated.  Doing so risks exposing admin access to the storage bucket, permitting malicious CORS metadata to be set and content served.

Signed-off-by: Chris Evich <cevich@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM once corrections are made. Thank you, the 'SDK' thing was helpful in understanding how to address this. But thanks most of all for finding and fixing the root cause of the problem.

@cevich The http -> https target is painted on my back.  That just became a possibility in ReadTheDocs, they didn't support it until a week or two ago and I flipped the switch.  So no aliens creeping around GitHub for that one.

Thanks for the honesty @TomSweeneyRedHat, no worries it's a completely understandable change.  Likely my bad for not including https in the list to begin with.  I'm just glad it didn't break because of some automation or file change (or aliens) :grinning:
